lem:admissible-row-factorization.proof_tex: Proof. Since C belongs to Mcal_m(G,u,v,I_v,Pi_v), def:cumulant-admissible-family applies. Outside the proper algebraic exceptional set in that definition, for each r there is a nonzero scalar A_r(Lambda,epsilon), independent of s, such that kappa_X(i_r,p_s,C_r) = A_r(B_Lambda)_{p_s,i_r} for every s and kappa_X(i_r,v,C_r) = A_r(B_Lambda)_{v,i_r}. By def:observable-transfer-matrix, the left side of the first equality is [K_X(I_v,C)]_{r,s}; by def:observable-transfer-vector, the left side of the second equality is [k_X(v;I_v,C)]_r. Thus the r-th row of K_X(I_v,C) is A_r times the r-th row of H, and the r-th entry of k_X(v;I_v,C) is A_r b_r. This is exactly K_X(I_v,C) = diag(A_1,...,A_q)H and k_X(v;I_v,C) = diag(A_1,...,A_q)b.

lem:witness-transfer-identity.proof_tex: Proof. From ass:linear-mixed-representation, B_Lambda = (I-Lambda)^(-T), so (I-Lambda)^T B_Lambda = I. Taking row v and column i_r gives (B_Lambda)_{v,i_r} - sum_{a in V} Lambda_{a v}(B_Lambda)_{a,i_r} = 1{v=i_r}. By the graph sparsity in ass:linear-mixed-representation, Lambda_{a v}=0 unless a is a parent of v, so the sum reduces to a in pa(v) = {p_1,...,p_q}. We now show that 1{v=i_r}=0. The TDE witness gives a directed path pi_r from i_r to p_r, and p_r is a parent of v. If i_r=v, then pi_r followed by the directed edge p_r -> v would form a directed cycle; if pi_r is the length-zero path, then p_r=i_r=v would make v a parent of itself, also impossible in an acyclic directed mixed graph. Thus i_r != v. Therefore (B_Lambda)_{v,i_r} = sum_{s=1}^q Lambda_{p_s v}(B_Lambda)_{p_s,i_r}. This is precisely b_r = (H lambda_Pv,v)_r for every r, and hence H lambda_Pv,v = b.

snapshot.stmt: Under ass:acyclic-admg, ass:linear-mixed-representation, and ass:tde-witness, if H_{r,s} = (B_Lambda)_{p_s,i_r} and b_r = (B_Lambda)_{v,i_r}, then H lambda_Pv,v = b.

lem:witness-minor-invertible.proof_tex: Proof. The TDE witness consists of a non-intersecting directed path system Pi_v pairing i_r to p_r. The associated v-rank witness minor is the determinant of the submatrix of B_Lambda with columns indexed by the witness nodes I_v and rows indexed by the parents P_v, equivalently det H up to transpose. Ass:genericity states that the directed coefficients and nonzero connected error cumulants lie outside the algebraic exceptional set on which the relevant witness minors vanish. Applying this to the witness minor supplied by Pi_v yields det H != 0 on the generic model class.

lem:second-cumulant-is-covariance.proof_tex: Proof. The second joint cumulant of two scalar random variables is cum(Y,Z) = E[YZ] - E[Y]E[Z]. Taking Y = X_a and Z = X_b gives kappa_X(a,b) = E[X_a X_b] - E[X_a]E[X_b] = Cov(X_a,X_b).

snapshot.stmt: For any two coordinates a and b for which the second cumulant is finite, kappa_X(a,b) = Cov(X_a,X_b).

lem:chosen-witness-order-not-information.proof_tex:
Let \(V_H=\{z,w,u,v\}\). Put directed edges \(z	o u\), \(w	o u\), and \(u	o v\), and put the single bidirected edge \(w\leftrightarrow v\). There are no other directed or bidirected edges. The directed part is acyclic. The target edge is \(u	o v\), so \(P_v=(u)\), \(q=1\), and we choose the witness tuple \(I_v=(w)\) with path system \(\Pi_v=(w	o u)\). For \(q=1\), the nonintersecting-path condition is just the existence of a directed path from the witness to the parent, and generically \((B_\Lambda)_{u,w}=\lambda_{wu}
e 0\), so this is a valid TDE rank witness.

Take the linear SEM
\[
X_z=\epsilon_z,\qquad X_w=\epsilon_w,\qquad X_u=\lambda_{zu}X_z+\lambda_{wu}X_w+\epsilon_u,\qquad X_v=\lambda_{uv}X_u+\epsilon_v.
\]
Choose finite-moment error laws satisfying the connected-set Markov property for the bidirected graph: the only off-diagonal second cumulant allowed to be generically nonzero is \(\omega_{wv}:=\kappa_\epsilon(w,v)\), while \(z\) is bidirected-disconnected from every other node and \(u\) is bidirected-isolated. Work on the generic open set where
\[
\lambda_{zu}\lambda_{wu}\sigma_z^2\sigma_w^2\omega_{wv}
e 0,\qquad\sigma_z^2=\kappa_\epsilon(z,z),\quad \sigma_w^2=\kappa_\epsilon(w,w).
\]
The path-transfer entries needed below are
\[
(B_\Lambda)_{u,w}=\lambda_{wu},\qquad (B_\Lambda)_{v,w}=\lambda_{uv}\lambda_{wu},\qquad (B_\Lambda)_{u,z}=\lambda_{zu},\qquad (B_\Lambda)_{v,z}=\lambda_{uv}\lambda_{zu}.
\]

First show that the order-two empty augmentation is not admissible for the chosen witness \(w\). Since \(m=2\) and \(q=1\), the only candidate family is \(C=(\emptyset)\). By multilinearity of cumulants and the connected-set Markov zeros,
\[
\kappa_X(w,u)=\kappa(\epsilon_w,\lambda_{zu}\epsilon_z+\lambda_{wu}\epsilon_w+\epsilon_u)=\lambda_{wu}\sigma_w^2,
\]
and
\[
\kappa_X(w,v)=\kappa(\epsilon_w,\lambda_{uv}X_u+\epsilon_v)=\lambda_{uv}\lambda_{wu}\sigma_w^2+\omega_{wv}.
\]
If \(C=(\emptyset)\) belonged to \(\mathcal M_2(G,u,v,I_v,\Pi_v)\), `def:cumulant-admissible-family` would give a scalar \(A
e0\) such that
\[
\kappa_X(w,u)=A(B_\Lambda)_{u,w},\qquad \kappa_X(w,v)=A(B_\Lambda)_{v,w}.
\]
The first equality and \(\lambda_{wu}
e0\) force \(A=\sigma_w^2\). The second equality would then force
\[
\lambda_{uv}\lambda_{wu}\sigma_w^2+\omega_{wv}=\lambda_{uv}\lambda_{wu}\sigma_w^2,
\]
so \(\omega_{wv}=0\), contradicting the generic choice. Hence \(C=(\emptyset)
otin\mathcal M_2(G,u,v,I_v,\Pi_v)\). Since it is the only order-two family, \(\mathcal M_2(G,u,v,I_v,\Pi_v)=\emptyset\), and by `def:minimal-order` we have \(2<m_\star(G,u,v,I_v,\Pi_v)\), allowing \(m_\star=\infty\).

Now show that order-two observable cumulants nevertheless identify the target coefficient on the same graph. Again by multilinearity and the connected-set Markov zeros involving \(z\),
\[
\kappa_X(z,u)=\kappa(\epsilon_z,\lambda_{zu}\epsilon_z+\lambda_{wu}\epsilon_w+\epsilon_u)=\lambda_{zu}\sigma_z^2,
\]
and
\[
\kappa_X(z,v)=\kappa(\epsilon_z,\lambda_{uv}X_u+\epsilon_v)=\lambda_{uv}\lambda_{zu}\sigma_z^2.
\]
On the generic set \(\lambda_{zu}\sigma_z^2
e0\),
\[
\lambda_{uv}= rac{\kappa_X(z,v)}{\kappa_X(z,u)}.
\]
Therefore any two generic laws in this model that agree on all observable cumulants up to order two agree in particular on \(\kappa_X(z,u)\) and \(\kappa_X(z,v)\), and the displayed ratio forces them to have the same \(\lambda_{uv}\). Thus no pair of generic laws can agree on all order-two observable cumulants and differ in \(\lambda_{uv}\). This proves that the chosen-witness minimal transfer order is not an information-theoretic lower frontier.

snapshot.stmt: There is an acyclic directed mixed graph satisfying the frozen structural assumptions and a chosen TDE witness (I_v,Pi_v) for which Mcal_2(G,u,v,I_v,Pi_v) is empty, hence 2 < m_star(G,u,v,I_v,Pi_v), while lambda_uv is identified from order-two observable cumulants. Therefore the witness-specific minimal transfer order m_star is not a lower-order indistinguishability frontier.

lem:acyclic-transfer-support.proof_tex:
Proof. Because the directed part of G is acyclic, choose a topological order. In that order the directed-edge matrix is nilpotent, so
B_Lambda=(I-Lambda)^(-T)=(I-Lambda^T)^(-1)=sum_{ell=0}^{|V|-1} (Lambda^T)^ell.
For ell >= 1, the (j,t) entry of (Lambda^T)^ell is a sum over directed paths of length ell from t to j, with each path weighted by the product of its directed coefficients. Thus no directed path from t to j implies (B_Lambda)_{j,t}=0. The ell=0 term is the identity matrix, so (B_Lambda)_{j,j}=1. If An(i)={i}, then no t != i has a directed path to i, hence X_i=sum_t (B_Lambda)_{i,t} epsilon_t=epsilon_i.

snapshot.stmt: In an acyclic linear mixed representation X = B_Lambda epsilon with B_Lambda = (I - Lambda)^(-T), the entry (B_Lambda)_{j,t} is zero unless t is a directed ancestor of j, and (B_Lambda)_{j,j}=1. Consequently, if An(i)={i}, then X_i=epsilon_i.

lem:source-isolated-second-cumulant-fact.proof_tex:
Proof. Fix r and j in P_v union {v}. By lem:acyclic-transfer-support, X_{i_r}=epsilon_{i_r}. Also X_j=sum_{t in V} (B_Lambda)_{j,t} epsilon_t. Bilinearity of the second cumulant gives
kappa_X(i_r,j)=kappa(epsilon_{i_r}, sum_t (B_Lambda)_{j,t} epsilon_t)=sum_t (B_Lambda)_{j,t} kappa_epsilon(i_r,t).
Again by lem:acyclic-transfer-support, (B_Lambda)_{j,t}=0 unless t is a directed ancestor of j. Since j belongs to P_v union {v}, every t with a possibly nonzero coefficient is an ancestor of some node in P_v union {v}. For t != i_r, the source-isolation condition says that i_r and t are bidirected-disconnected. The connected-set Markov property for bidirected errors says that a mixed error cumulant indexed by a bidirected-disconnected set is zero; at order two this gives kappa_epsilon(i_r,t)=0 for every such t != i_r. Therefore the sum has only the t=i_r contribution, namely kappa_X(i_r,j)=(B_Lambda)_{j,i_r} kappa_epsilon(i_r,i_r).

snapshot.stmt: Assume the source-isolation condition in thm:source-isolated-covariance-admissible: each i_r is a directed source with An(i_r)={i_r} and, for every distinct t that is an ancestor of some node in P_v union {v}, i_r is bidirected-disconnected from t. Then for every r and every j in P_v union {v}, kappa_X(i_r,j)=kappa_epsilon(i_r,i_r)(B_Lambda)_{j,i_r} on the model class where the displayed second cumulants are defined.

The connected-set Markov property for bidirected errors says that a mixed error cumulant indexed by a bidirected-disconnected set is zero; at order two this gives kappa_epsilon(i_r,t)=0 for every such t != i_r. Therefore the sum has only the t=i_r contribution, namely kappa_X(i_r,j)=(B_Lambda)_{j,i_r} kappa_epsilon(i_r,i_r).

lem:source-isolated-empty-family-admissi.proof_tex:
Proof. Let C_r be the empty tuple for every r, so C is an order-2 family. Define A_r=kappa_epsilon(i_r,i_r). By ass:genericity, the generic parameter set excludes the algebraic hypersurfaces on which the relevant connected error cumulants vanish; in particular A_r != 0 generically for every witness row r.

Apply lem:source-isolated-second-cumulant-factorization first with j=p_s for s=1,...,q and then with j=v. We obtain
kappa_X(i_r,p_s)=A_r (B_Lambda)_{p_s,i_r},
kappa_X(i_r,v)=A_r (B_Lambda)_{v,i_r}.
The scalar A_r depends on r and on the model parameters, but it is independent of the parent column s. These are exactly the row-uniform factorization requirements in def:cumulant-admissible-family for the empty order-2 family. Hence C belongs to Mcal_2(G,u,v,I_v,Pi_v).

The displayed matrix identities now follow from def:observable-transfer-matrix and def:observable-transfer-vector: the (r,s) entry of K_X(I_v,C) is kappa_X(i_r,p_s)=A_r H_{r,s}, and the r-th entry of k_X(v;I_v,C) is kappa_X(i_r,v)=A_r b_r.

oeq:finite-order-transfer.proof_tex:
Negative resolution. The search handle does not always terminate from the frozen assumptions. It suffices to give one acyclic ADMG satisfying the TDE witness condition for which no finite admissible family exists. Let V={1,2,3,4}. Let the directed edges be 1->2, 1->3, 2->4, and 3->4, and let the bidirected edges be 1<->2, 2<->4, and 3<->4. Take the target edge u->v to be 2->4. Order the parent tuple as P_4=(3,2), so q=2 and s_u=2. Let I_4=(1,2), with path system Pi_4 consisting of the directed path 1->3 and the trivial path 2->2. The graph is acyclic. Moreover Sib(4)={2,3,4}, Sib(1)={1,2}, Sib(2)={1,2,4}, and Sib(3)={3,4}, so the removable-ancestor set for node 4 in the TDE criterion is R_4={1,2}. The displayed paths are vertex-disjoint and pair I_4 to P_4. Equivalently, after writing a=lambda_12, b=lambda_13, c=lambda_24, and d=lambda_34, the hidden witness submatrix B_{P_4,I_4} is [[b,0],[a,1]], whose determinant is b and is generically nonzero. Thus this instance satisfies the body of ass:tde-witness, and the TDE v-rank criterion certifies generic identifiability of the two parent coefficients of node 4, in particular lambda_24.

Keep the notation a=lambda_12, b=lambda_13, c=lambda_24, and d=lambda_34. From X=B_Lambda epsilon with B_Lambda=(I-Lambda)^(-T), the directed-path transfer matrix is

B_Lambda = [[1,0,0,0],[a,1,0,0],[b,0,1,0],[ac+bd,c,d,1]].

In particular B_{3,2}=0, while B_{w,1} is the polynomial 1, a, b, or ac+bd according as w=1,2,3,4. Each of these four polynomials is generically nonzero after excluding a proper algebraic set.

Now fix an arbitrary cumulant order m>=2 and an arbitrary candidate row augmentation C_2=(c_1,...,c_{m-2}) in V^{m-2} for the second witness row i_2=2. Consider the column j=3. By multilinearity of joint cumulants and X_w=sum_t B_{w,t} epsilon_t,

kappa_X(2,3,C_2) = sum_{t_0,...,t_{m-1} in V} B_{2,t_0} B_{3,t_1} prod_{ell=1}^{m-2} B_{c_ell,t_{ell+1}} kappa_epsilon(t_0,t_1,t_2,...,t_{m-1}).

The summand with t_0=t_1=...=t_{m-1}=1 is

a b prod_{ell=1}^{m-2} B_{c_ell,1} kappa_epsilon(1,1,...,1).

The singleton set {1} is connected in the bidirected part, so ass:connected-set-markov imposes no zero restriction on the cumulant coordinate kappa_epsilon(1,1,...,1). The coefficient a b prod_ell B_{c_ell,1} is a nonzero polynomial in a,b,c,d. Hence kappa_X(2,3,C_2) is not the zero polynomial in the directed coefficients and connected error cumulants; it is nonzero on a Zariski-open generic subset of the model class.

But if C belonged to Mcal_m(G,2,4,I_4,Pi_4), then the second row of def:cumulant-admissible-family would require, for the parent p_1=3,

kappa_X(i_2,p_1,C_2) = A_2(Lambda,epsilon) (B_Lambda)_{p_1,i_2} = A_2(Lambda,epsilon) B_{3,2} = 0.

This contradicts the generic nonvanishing just proved. Since the argument used an arbitrary order m and an arbitrary C_2 in V^{m-2}, no family C can be admissible at any finite order. Therefore Mcal_m(G,2,4,I_4,Pi_4) is empty for every m>=2, so m_star(G,2,4,I_4,Pi_4)=infinity for this TDE-certified instance. Thus the answer to the open-ended question is no: ass:acyclic-admg, ass:linear-mixed-representation, ass:connected-set-markov, ass:tde-witness, and ass:genericity do not force the search handle in def:transfer-search-handle to terminate.

thm:cumulant-row-system.proof_tex: Proof. Work on the generic subset on which the admissibility factorization for C holds and the TDE witness minor is nonzero. Let H be the q by q hidden witness-transfer matrix H_{r,s} = (B_Lambda)_{p_s,i_r}, and let b be the q-vector b_r = (B_Lambda)_{v,i_r}. By lem:admissible-row-factorization, there are nonzero row scalars A_1,...,A_q such that K_X(I_v,C) = D_A H and k_X(v;I_v,C) = D_A b, where D_A = diag(A_1,...,A_q). By lem:witness-transfer-identity, H lambda_Pv,v = b. Therefore K_X(I_v,C) lambda_Pv,v = D_A H lambda_Pv,v = D_A b = k_X(v;I_v,C). It remains to check nonsingularity. By lem:witness-minor-invertible, det H != 0 generically. Since every A_r is nonzero on the same generic set, det K_X(I_v,C) = det(D_A H) = (prod_{r=1}^q A_r) det H != 0. This proves both the observable row system and the generic denominator condition.

thm:closed-form-coefficient.proof_tex: Proof. Under thm:cumulant-row-system, K_X(I_v,C) is nonsingular and K_X(I_v,C) lambda_Pv,v = k_X(v;I_v,C). Hence lambda_Pv,v is the unique solution x of K_X(I_v,C)x = k_X(v;I_v,C). Expanding det(K_X with column s replaced by k_X) along the standard alternating multilinear determinant in the s-th column, or equivalently applying Cramer's rule, gives x_s = det(K_X with column s replaced by k_X)/det K_X for every s = 1,...,q. Taking s = s_u and using p_{s_u}=u, we obtain x_{s_u} = Lambda_{u v} = lambda_uv. Thus lambda_uv equals det(K_X(I_v,C) with column s_u replaced by k_X(v;I_v,C)) divided by det K_X(I_v,C).

prop:witness-invariance.proof_tex: Proof. Let R_C be the determinant ratio built from the admissible family C, and let R_{C'} be the analogous ratio built from C'. On the generic domain where the denominator for C is nonzero, thm:closed-form-coefficient gives R_C = lambda_uv. On the generic domain where the denominator for C' is nonzero, the same theorem gives R_{C'} = lambda_uv. The intersection of these two generic domains is again generic, since it is obtained by deleting the union of two proper algebraic exceptional sets. On this intersection R_C = lambda_uv = R_{C'}. Both ratios are rational functions of the observable cumulants, pulled back to rational functions on the generic model parameter space. Two rational functions that agree on a nonempty Zariski-open subset of their common domain agree as rational functions on the generic model class. Hence the determinant ratios coincide as rational functions on the generic model class.

prop:iv-reduction.proof_tex: Proof. If q = 1 and P_v = (u), then s_u = 1, K_X(I_v,C) is the 1 by 1 matrix with entry kappa_X(i_1,u,C_1), and k_X(v;I_v,C) is the one-vector with entry kappa_X(i_1,v,C_1). For m = 2 and the empty tuple C_1, these entries are kappa_X(i_1,u) and kappa_X(i_1,v). The determinant ratio in thm:closed-form-coefficient is therefore kappa_X(i_1,v)/kappa_X(i_1,u). By lem:second-cumulant-is-covariance, kappa_X(i_1,a) = Cov(X_{i_1},X_a) for a in {u,v}. Hence the order-2 formula is lambda_uv = Cov(X_{i_1},X_v)/Cov(X_{i_1},X_u), with the denominator nonzero on the generic domain supplied by thm:closed-form-coefficient.

oeq:minimal-order-sharpness.proof_tex:
The requested sharpness statement has a negative answer under the frozen core. The obstruction is that \(m_\star(G,u,v,I_v,\Pi_v)\) is a witness-specific transfer order, while lower-order observable cumulants may identify \(\lambda_{uv}\) by a different witness or a different formula. Thus \(m_\star\) is not, in general, an information-theoretic lower frontier for all cumulant functionals.

The concrete counterexample is `lem:chosen-witness-order-not-information-frontier`. In that graph the chosen TDE witness is \(I_v=(w)\) for the target edge \(u	o v\). The order-two empty augmentation is not admissible for this chosen witness, because the bidirected edge \(w\leftrightarrow v\) contributes the nonzero covariance term \(\kappa_\epsilon(w,v)\) to \(\kappa_X(w,v)\) without contributing to \(B_{v,w}\). Hence \(\mathcal M_2(G,u,v,I_v,\Pi_v)=\emptyset\), so \(2<m_\star(G,u,v,I_v,\Pi_v)\) with the convention that every finite integer is smaller than \(\infty\).

At the same time the same graph contains a second observed node \(z\) with directed path \(z	o u	o v\) and no bidirected connection to the remaining nodes. For every generic law in this model,
\[
\kappa_X(z,u)=\lambda_{zu}\sigma_z^2,\qquad
\kappa_X(z,v)=\lambda_{uv}\lambda_{zu}\sigma_z^2,
\]
with \(\lambda_{zu}\sigma_z^2
e 0\). Therefore
\[
\lambda_{uv}= rac{\kappa_X(z,v)}{\kappa_X(z,u)}.
\]
This ratio uses only order-two observable cumulants. Consequently, two generic laws on this graph that agree on all observable cumulants up to order two must have the same value of \(\lambda_{uv}\). The proposed lower-order indistinguishability clause, applied at \(m=2<m_\star\), is false.

What remains universally true is only the construction-level statement built into `def:minimal-order`: if \(h<m_\star(G,u,v,I_v,\Pi_v)\), then no order-\(h\) family belongs to \(\mathcal M_h(G,u,v,I_v,\Pi_v)\). This follows directly from the definition of the minimum over the nonempty admissible-order set, with the same convention when the set is empty and \(m_\star=\infty\). But this conclusion rules out only the row-uniform cumulant-transfer design associated with the chosen witness. It does not rule out other order-\(h\) observable rational functionals, and therefore it cannot yield the requested two-law indistinguishability result.

Thus the answer to the OEQ is: no, the frozen \(m_\star\) cannot be characterized as a sharp lower cumulant-identification order for \(\lambda_{uv}\) from the stated primitives. A true sharp-order theorem would need a different object, namely an information order minimizing over all observable cumulant functionals or an additional global lower-bound theorem; neither is contained in `def:minimal-order` or the stated assumptions.

prop:plugin-delta-method.proof_tex:
Proof. Fix the admissible pair (m,C). Let T be the finite vector of all distinct population cumulants kappa_X(i_r,p_s,C_r) and kappa_X(i_r,v,C_r) entering K_X(I_v,C) and k_X(v;I_v,C), and let T_hat_n be the corresponding vector of empirical k-statistics entering Khat_n(I_v,C) and khat_n(v;I_v,C). Let g be the rational map that sends a cumulant vector t to det(K(t) with column s_u replaced by k(t))/det K(t). By def:plugin-estimator, lambdahat_uv,n(C) = g(T_hat_n) on the event det Khat_n(I_v,C) != 0.

By ass:denominator-margin, |det K(T)| >= c0 > 0. Hence det K(t) stays nonzero on an open neighborhood of T, and g is continuously differentiable there. By lem:sample-cumulant-clt-delta and ass:finite-moments, T_hat_n ->p T and sqrt(n)(T_hat_n - T) ->d N(0,Sigma_T), where Sigma_T is the limiting covariance matrix of the empirical cumulant vector. Continuity of g gives g(T_hat_n) ->p g(T). The closed-form coefficient theorem gives g(T)=lambda_uv, so lambdahat_uv,n(C) ->p lambda_uv.

The same finite-dimensional delta-method lemma gives
sqrt(n)(g(T_hat_n)-g(T)) ->d N(0, grad g(T)^T Sigma_T grad g(T)).
Therefore sqrt(n)(lambdahat_uv,n(C)-lambda_uv) converges in distribution to a mean-zero normal law with variance grad g(T)^T Sigma_T grad g(T), precisely the multivariate delta-method variance for the sample cumulants entering Khat_n(I_v,C) and khat_n(v;I_v,C). Finally, because det Khat_n(I_v,C) ->p det K_X(I_v,C) and |det K_X(I_v,C)| >= c0, the event det Khat_n(I_v,C) != 0 has probability tending to one; any convention for the estimator on its complement is asymptotically irrelevant.

snapshot.stmt: Fix m >= 2 and C in Mcal_m(G,u,v,I_v,Pi_v). Under ass:finite-moments and ass:denominator-margin, lambdahat_uv,n(C) converges in probability to lambda_uv and sqrt(n) (lambdahat_uv,n(C) - lambda_uv) converges in distribution to a mean-zero normal law whose variance is obtained by the multivariate delta method applied to the vector of sample cumulants entering Khat_n(I_v,C) and khat_n(v;I_v,C).

defs.def:plugin-estimator: lambdahat_uv,n(C) = det(Khat_n(I_v,C) with column s_u replaced by khat_n(v;I_v,C)) / det Khat_n(I_v,C).

assumptions.ass:finite-moments: All joint moments of X up to order 2m are finite.
assumptions.ass:denominator-margin: For the chosen admissible pair (m,C), |det K_X(I_v,C)| >= c0 for some constant c0 > 0.

lem:sample-cumulant-clt-delta.proof_tex:
Proof. Let the finitely many cumulant arguments be J_1,...,J_d, with |J_l| <= m. For every block B appearing in a partition of any J_l, introduce the raw moment mu_B=E[prod_{a in B} X_a]. Only finitely many such raw moments are needed, and each has total degree at most m. Let mu be the resulting finite raw-moment vector and let mu_hat_n be its empirical analogue formed from the n i.i.d. observations of X.

The joint cumulant of an ordered tuple J is the finite partition polynomial
\[
\kappa_X(J)=\sum_{\pi\in\Pi(J)} (|\pi|-1)!(-1)^{|\pi|-1}\prod_{B\in\pi} E\left[\prod_{a\in B}X_a\right].
\]
Thus the population cumulant vector is F(mu) for a polynomial map F. Ass:finite-moments states that all joint moments of X up to order 2m are finite. Hence every monomial entering mu has a finite second moment, because its square has total degree at most 2m. The ordinary finite-dimensional law of large numbers and central limit theorem for the empirical average vector mu_hat_n therefore give
\[
mu_hat_n ->p mu, \qquad \sqrt n(mu_hat_n-mu) ->d N(0,Omega)
\]
for the finite covariance matrix Omega of the raw-moment monomial vector.

The plug-in cumulant vector F(mu_hat_n) is consequently consistent, and the first-order Taylor expansion of the polynomial F gives
\[
\sqrt n\{F(mu_hat_n)-F(mu)\} ->d N(0, DF(mu)Omega DF(mu)^T).
\]
It remains only to pass from plug-in cumulants to the empirical k-statistics named in the frozen core. For fixed cumulant order, each k-statistic is a finite-sample unbiased polynomial in the empirical power sums whose coefficients are rational functions of n and whose coefficientwise limit is the corresponding plug-in cumulant polynomial. Therefore, for the fixed finite list J_1,...,J_d, the difference between the k-statistic vector T_hat_n and F(mu_hat_n) is O_p(n^{-1}); the empirical raw moments appearing in those polynomials are O_p(1) by the preceding law of large numbers. Hence sqrt(n){T_hat_n-F(mu_hat_n)} ->p 0. Slutsky's theorem gives T_hat_n ->p T and
\[
\sqrt n(T_hat_n-T) ->d N(0,Sigma_T),\qquad Sigma_T=DF(mu)Omega DF(mu)^T.
\]
Finally, if g is continuously differentiable in a neighborhood of T, Taylor's formula gives g(T_hat_n)-g(T)=Dg(T)(T_hat_n-T)+o_p(||T_hat_n-T||). Since T_hat_n-T=O_p(n^{-1/2}), multiplying by sqrt(n) and applying Slutsky's theorem yields the stated delta-method normal limit with variance Dg(T) Sigma_T Dg(T)^T.

snapshot.stmt: Fix a finite list of joint cumulants of coordinates of X, each of order at most m, and let T be the population cumulant vector and T_hat_n the corresponding vector of empirical k-statistics computed from n i.i.d. observations of X. Under ass:finite-moments, T_hat_n converges in probability to T and sqrt(n)(T_hat_n-T) converges in distribution to a mean-zero multivariate normal vector. Moreover, if g is continuously differentiable in a neighborhood of T, then sqrt(n)(g(T_hat_n)-g(T)) converges in distribution to the mean-zero normal law with variance Dg(T) Sigma_T Dg(T)^T, where Sigma_T is the covariance matrix of the limiting sample-cumulant vector.

assumptions.ass:finite-moments: All joint moments of X up to order 2m are finite.

thm:source-isolated-covariance-admissibl.proof_tex:
Proof. Work on the source-isolated subclass described in the statement, and let C be the order-2 empty family, so C_r is the empty tuple for every r. By lem:source-isolated-empty-family-admissible, this C belongs to Mcal_2(G,u,v,I_v,Pi_v). The same lemma gives the advertised observable row factorization: if H_{r,s}=(B_Lambda)_{p_s,i_r}, b_r=(B_Lambda)_{v,i_r}, and D=diag(kappa_epsilon(i_1,i_1),...,kappa_epsilon(i_q,i_q)), then K_X(I_v,C)=D H and k_X(v;I_v,C)=D b. This proves the concrete covariance-transfer display in the theorem.

Since m=2 and C is admissible, thm:cumulant-row-system applies on the generic model class. It yields K_X(I_v,C) lambda_Pv,v = k_X(v;I_v,C) and det K_X(I_v,C) != 0. Therefore K_X(I_v,C) is invertible and lambda_Pv,v = K_X(I_v,C)^{-1} k_X(v;I_v,C). This identifies the whole parent-effect vector from observable second cumulants.

For the target coefficient, apply thm:closed-form-coefficient to the same admissible pair (2,C). The s_u coordinate of lambda_Pv,v is lambda_uv because p_{s_u}=u, hence
lambda_uv = det(K_X(I_v,C) with column s_u replaced by k_X(v;I_v,C)) / det K_X(I_v,C).
All entries in this determinant ratio are observable order-2 cumulants, equivalently covariances by lem:second-cumulant-is-covariance whenever the second moments are finite.

By lem:order-two-admissible-implies-minimal-order-two, the nonemptiness of Mcal_2(G,u,v,I_v,Pi_v) implies m_star(G,u,v,I_v,Pi_v)=2, because the search domain begins at order 2. Finally suppose q=1 and P_v=(u). Then K_X(I_v,C) is the 1 by 1 matrix [kappa_X(i_1,u)] and k_X(v;I_v,C) is [kappa_X(i_1,v)]. The determinant ratio becomes kappa_X(i_1,v)/kappa_X(i_1,u), which equals Cov(X_{i_1},X_v)/Cov(X_{i_1},X_u) by lem:second-cumulant-is-covariance. Thus prop:iv-reduction is exactly the one-parent special case of this source-isolated covariance theorem.

snapshot.stmt: Suppose ass:acyclic-admg, ass:linear-mixed-representation, ass:connected-set-markov, ass:tde-witness, and ass:genericity hold, and in addition each witness row i_r (r=1,...,q) is a directed SOURCE in G with An(i_r)={i_r} and is bidirected-disconnected from every error node that is an ancestor of some node in P_v union {v}. Then the empty order-2 family C with C_r = empty tuple for all r belongs to Mcal_2(G,u,v,I_v,Pi_v); concretely K_X(I_v,C) = diag(kappa_epsilon(i_1,i_1),...,kappa_epsilon(i_q,i_q)) H with H_{r,s}=(B_Lambda)_{p_s,i_r} and k_X(v;I_v,C)=diag(kappa_epsilon(i_r,i_r)) b with b_r=(B_Lambda)_{v,i_r}. Consequently, on the generic model class, det K_X(I_v,C) != 0 and lambda_Pv,v = K_X(I_v,C)^{-1} k_X(v;I_v,C), so by thm:closed-form-coefficient the entire parent-effect vector of v, in particular lambda_uv, is identified by the observable ORDER-2 (covariance) determinant ratio. Hence on the source-isolated witness subclass the observable identifying functional exists UNCONDITIONALLY at covariance order (m_star=2), and prop:iv-reduction is the q=1 special case.

defs.def:cumulant-admissible-family: { C = (C_1,...,C_q) in (V^{m-2})^q : outside a proper algebraic subset of parameter pairs (Lambda,epsilon) satisfying ass:linear-mixed-representation and ass:connected-set-markov on G, for each r in {1,...,q} there exists a nonzero scalar A_r(Lambda,epsilon) independent of s such that kappa_X(i_r,p_s,C_r) = A_r(Lambda,epsilon) (B_Lambda)_{p_s,i_r} for every s in {1,...,q} and kappa_X(i_r,v,C_r) = A_r(Lambda,epsilon) (B_Lambda)_{v,i_r} }

assumptions.ass:acyclic-admg: G is an acyclic directed mixed graph on V and u -> v is a directed edge in G.
assumptions.ass:linear-mixed-representation: X = B_Lambda epsilon with B_Lambda = (I - Lambda)^(-T) and Lambda_ab = 0 whenever a -> b is absent from G.
assumptions.ass:connected-set-markov: The error vector epsilon satisfies the connected-set Markov property with respect to the bidirected part of G.
assumptions.ass:tde-witness: There exist an ordered witness tuple I_v and a non-intersecting path system Pi_v pairing i_r to p_r for r = 1,...,q, so the TDE v-rank criterion certifies generic identifiability of lambda_uv.
assumptions.ass:genericity: The directed coefficients and the nonzero connected error cumulants lie outside the algebraic exceptional set on which the relevant witness minors vanish.

lem:order-two-admissible-implies-minimal.proof_tex: Proof. By def:minimal-order, m_star(G,u,v,I_v,Pi_v) is the minimum over the set of integers m >= 2 for which Mcal_m(G,u,v,I_v,Pi_v) is nonempty, with value infinity only if this set is empty. If Mcal_2(G,u,v,I_v,Pi_v) is nonempty, then 2 belongs to this set. No integer smaller than 2 belongs to the search domain. Hence the minimum is exactly 2.

snapshot.stmt: If Mcal_2(G,u,v,I_v,Pi_v) is nonempty, then m_star(G,u,v,I_v,Pi_v)=2.

defs.def:minimal-order: m_star(G,u,v,I_v,Pi_v) = min{ m >= 2 : Mcal_m(G,u,v,I_v,Pi_v) != emptyset }, with value infinity if the set is empty.

node.statement: If Mcal_2(G,u,v,I_v,Pi_v) is nonempty, then m_star(G,u,v,I_v,Pi_v)=2.
